body:
This PR:
 - Adds the `config` CL option, and uses it for the column radiation case
 - Combines `make_distributed_horizontal_space` and `make_horizontal_space` and use dispatch
 - inlines some included contents in `single_column_radiative_equilibrium.jl`
 - Adds a TODO for a bug I found in the restart block:
```
    # TODO:   quad, horizontal_mesh, z_stretch,
    #         z_max, z_elem should be taken from Y.
```
Otherwise restarting can result in inconsistent grid data.

This is another small step towards removing the `TEST_NAME` flag.
title: Add config flag, move column parts to driver

body:
This PR:
 - Deletes and inlines `single_column_radiative_equilibrium.jl`
 - Removes a global closure over `FT`
 - Changes `t_end` from `60 * 60 * 24 * 365.25` to `60 * 60 * 24 * 365` in `single_column_radiative_equilibrium.jl` (for simplicity). Perhaps we could add an option `n_days` and use that or `t_end`, it may just be a nicer interface for users

I'm not really satisfied with `center_initial_condition_column` / `center_initial_condition_sphere`, since I don't think that's really an accurate characterization, I'll need help on improving names here. For now this will help make the next steps easier. Also, we're stacking up quite a few args for the radiative equilibrium, and we could handle this differently by having column-specific defaults (kind of like what I did with `dt_rad`), which may alleviate the number of args. I think we can do this in a follow up PR, once managing the different configs is a bit more elegant.

This is one more step towards removing `TEST_NAME`.
title: Prep for removing TEST_NAME

body:
The documentation is currently broken (everything is passing because we're not running in strict mode). This PR:
 - Removes `jldoctest; `
 - Enforces `strict = true` so that they don't go stale again
 - Adds docs for initial conditions in the API
 - Adds submodule docs in the API

I'm happy to fix the `jldoctest`s in this PR if someone is willing to help, otherwise I'll open an issue to add the tests back in, and get to it when possible.

I was hoping to add some docs for the command line options that automatically sync, so that we only need to maintain a single list that is always up to date (cc @jiahe23)
title: Fix documentation, make docs strict

body:
This PR builds ontop of #414 and auto-documents the CL arguments. Here's an example of the output on one of the newly added [doc page](https://clima.github.io/ClimaAtmos.jl/previews/PR415/cl_args/).

I think there's ways that we can improve formatting. It might be nice if we point to this page in the `examples/sphere/README.md`. That way we have a single consistent command line argument list. I'm open to moving that entire readme into the docs proper, too. Let's follow that up in a subsequent PR.
title: Auto-document CL arguments
